title: Symbiodinium-enriched RNA extraction from Aiptasia holobiont
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"231v6","text":"This protocol enriches holobiont Exaiptasia pallida tissue for Symbiodinium, removing more than 99% of host tissue while maintaining high RNA integrity (RIN \u003e 7). The symbiont-enriched fraction is subsequently lysed via bead beating and rna isolated using the Qiagen RNeasy Mini kit.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":33,"length":18},{"style":"italic","offset":63,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8qdv9","text":"This protocol was developed using small (~0.2 mm oral disc) anemones for extraction. With anemones hosting heterologous symbionts at low density (e.g. S. trenchii), pooling multiple anemones for each extraction is useful for increasing yield.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":151,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"51kf9","text":"Prior to starting, make the host lysis buffer as follows:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ec0sk","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"489rg","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":4,"length":9},{"style":"bold","offset":17,"length":19},{"style":"bold","offset":40,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"2gs12","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5immu","text":"Add 2-mercaptoethanol to a total concentration of 1% (v/v) to the volume of buffer that will be used within 24 hours. Prior to the addition of 2-mercaptoethanol, the buffer is stable at room temperature.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","0_2":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","1_2":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","2_2":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","3_2":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","4_2":""},"colWidths":[null,null,null],"data":[["Component ","Final Concentration ","Amount for 100 ml buffer "],["Guanidinium isothiocyanate (dry) ","4 M ","46.26 g "],["Sodium citrate (1 M stock soln) ","25 mM ","2.5 ml "],["Sarcosyl (10% stock soln) ","0.5% ","5 ml "],["Nuclease-free water "," ","To 100 ml total volume "]],"guid":"F7DA0C20458511E9BBB8AB3DE805F663","legend":"","mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[],"show_headers":true}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"8o9cf","text":"Host lysis buffer is hazardous in case of skin contact (irritant), eye contact (irritant), ingestion, and inhalation. Use appropriate protection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Freeze anemones in minimal seawater in 2 ml cryotubes in liquid nitrogen and store at -80°C till extraction.
Add 500 ul of host lysis buffer (with 2-mercaptoethanol) to tube containing the frozen anemone and flicked till frozen tissue is dislodged. Transfer to a 1.5 ml centrifuge tube by decanting.
Gently homogenize tissue with a plastic pestle. Vortex at maximum speed for 1 minute. Centrifuge homogenate at 10,000 g for 3 min. Remove and discard supernatant, leaving the symbiont pellet intact.
Resuspend pellet in 1.4 ml of host lysis buffer and vortex till homogenized. Centrifuge suspension at 3,000 g for 3 min.
Discard clear supernatant and add 1.4 ml of host lysis buffer. Vortexed at max till homogenous. Centrifuge at 3,000 g for 3 minutes and discard clear supernatant.

Resuspend Symbiodinium pellet in 600 ul Buffer RLT (Qiagen), transfer to a screw top 2.0 ml tube, and bead beat for 1 min at 6 m/s with approximately 100 μl glass beads. (Beads can be added to tubes and autoclaved in advance.)

Centrifuge the lysate for 3 min at 14000 g. Carefully remove the cleared lysate supernatant by pipetting, and transfer it to a new microcentrifuge tube. Process the cleared lysate with the Qiagen RNeasy Mini Kit as per manufacturer instructions (with on-column DNAse treatment), beginning with the addition of 1 volume of 70% ethanol prior to loading the sample on the supplied columns. In order to eliminate salt contamination in the final sample, users may perform an additional RPE Buffer wash and/or 80% ethanol wash prior to elution of total RNA.
creator: Anthony Bellantuono
